extra.reason: Closed

Shipped as part of the gallery-model rework: Designs list is scrollable past ~8 rows with a live count in the heading. Search/filter and thumbnails deliberately deferred until double-digit libraries are the norm.

Shipped: four striped combos (yellow/green, orange/red, pink, purple) as palette entries with a colors array; pinwheel wedge rendering with per-peg rotation on the board, conic-gradient swatches, unchanged pattern JSON format. Demo center ring now uses striped orange/red matching the original photo.

Shipped: two-finger tap undo / three-finger tap redo over the board stage, touch-only (pen and mouse paint). Accidental first-finger stroke reverts when a second finger joins, matching Procreate. Movement/slow-hold/pointercancel veto. Clear-board gesture deliberately omitted — destructive action stays behind the button.
Fixed: delta-gated autosave + session-frozen ordering; verified in headless Chrome

Visibility-gap reorder shipped: listOrder resets when tab becomes visible after ≥20 min hidden; short hides keep frozen order. Verified in headless Chrome (10/10 assertions incl. short-hide/long-hide paths).

Filled Build & Test (no-build constraint, tools/ usage, embed-examples rule, Pages deploy + flake caveat), Architecture Overview (single-file principle, gallery model, known vd2 duplication), and Conventions (zero-deps, file:// rules, determinism, comment style, destructive-action policy, RMW storage).

tools/core.js is now the single source of truth for pegXY, symCopies, expandBeads, and connectivity (contact constants 1.15/1.06). Embedded into index.html via new tools/embed-core.js (CORE markers, same pattern as embed-examples); inspect.js requires it directly. Bonus: inspect.js gains square-grid symmetry expansion and invalid-bead skipping it never had.

test/core.test.js, 18 tests on node's built-in runner (node --test, zero deps): symmetry orbits (polar+square, fold/mirror/non-divisible), expandBeads validation incl. snowflake wedge->127-bead integration fixture, connectivity at both contact thresholds incl. a stagger-only case, and an embed-drift test that fails if index.html's embedded core doesn't match tools/core.js.

tools/quantize.js shipped: PNG → area-average sampling in linear RGB → CIELAB k-means (deterministic, fixed seed) → nearest solid catalog color, no dithering. Supports square + polar boards, --sym orbit majority-voting, --bg auto/#hex background removal, --drop-islands via the shared connectivity check. tools/png.js is a zero-dep PNG codec (decode 8-bit gray/RGB/RGBA/palette; encode RGBA). Palette moved from index.html into tools/core.js as part of this. 9 new tests (27 total). Verified end-to-end: synthetic heart image → 320-bead 2-color buildable pattern rendered correctly in the app via preview.js.

From image button in the Design panel: canvas-decoded (PNG/JPEG/WebP/HEIC), downscaled to ≤1024px, quantized onto the current board via PerlerCore.quantizeImage — the pipeline extracted from the CLI into the shared core, so app and tools/quantize.js run identical code (CLI is now a thin png.js+flags wrapper). Defaults: 8 colors, bg auto for opaque images, alpha mask otherwise; creates a new design named after the file. patternJSON also moved to core (app currentJSON + CLI both use it). 3 new tests incl. CLI-vs-direct parity (30 total). Browser end-to-end verified via headless-Chrome harness: canvas heart -> importImagePixels -> 320 beads, byte-identical to the CLI result.

decodeImageFile now decodes through an element instead of createImageBitmap — HTML image decoding applies EXIF orientation by default in all modern engines, while createImageBitmap's handling is inconsistent. Verified end-to-end in headless Chrome with a synthetic phone-style JPEG (pixels stored 90° CCW + EXIF Orientation=6): the pattern imports upright.

core.pegXY polar angle now matches the renderer (index 0 at 12 o'clock, clockwise); the renderer's pegPositions refactored to derive from core.pegXY so the conventions can't drift again. Regression tests: pegXY convention assertions + a polar quantize orientation test (top-of-image must land at ring index 0). Verified with the user's actual mushroom screenshot imported onto circle-14 in headless Chrome — renders upright.

Phone pass shipped: (1) Procreate-style two-finger drag pan/zoom on the board (1x-8x, anchored at the pinch midpoint, snaps back at ~1x, survives undo/redo/theme rebuilds of the same board; two-finger tap undo & three-finger redo unchanged; SVG export always renders the full board, not the zoom window). (2) ≤600px CSS: sidebar 38vh + tighter chrome, JSON panel collapses to a peek strip that expands on focus. Verified headless at 500px (narrowest headless Chrome allows): no horizontal overflow at any width, zoom math confirmed via synthetic pinch screenshots; desktop layout unchanged. Real-device iOS Safari confirmation still worthwhile — reopen if painting precision or layout misbehaves on hardware.

Board-first phone layout: slim top bar (drawer menu, live design name, undo/redo), board maximized, palette as horizontally-scrollable bottom strip with erase toggle (thumb zone), all secondary functionality (design mgmt, designs list, board setup, symmetry, counts, JSON/export) in a slide-out drawer with dimmed overlay. Single palette/jsonPanel DOM nodes relocate between desktop and phone homes on matchMedia flips — no duplication. Verified at 500px headless (closed + drawer open); desktop/iPad unchanged.

Tapping a bead with its already-selected color now erases it; paint-vs-erase decided once per stroke at the start peg (pixel-editor convention) so drags never flicker. Chose toggle-to-empty over revert-to-previous-color: empty is a predictable visible state, revert needs invisible per-peg history and creates color ping-pong; undo covers repaint regret.

Share shipped: ↗ Share (Design panel + phone top bar) encodes {v, name, pattern} as gzip+base64url in the URL fragment (snowflake = 364 chars). Web Share API platforms get the native sheet with a client-rendered PNG of the board attached plus the link in the text; fallback copies the link. Opening #d= links decodes, validates, dedupes against identical saved patterns, saves to the gallery, and clears the hash. Codec in the shared core with 3 unit tests (round-trip, compression, garbage rejection); receive flow verified end-to-end in headless Chrome.

Graduated share payload via canShare: {files, text, url} preferred (dedicated url field renders as a real link), then {files, text-with-embedded-url}, then {title, url}. Share text now says the link opens an editable copy.

Two-layer fix: (1) tokens now end in a pad-count digit ('G'/'R' format) so messaging-app linkifiers can never trim the tail — this was the actual IMG_1635 failure ('-'/'_' at token end trimmed from the tappable link, losing the gzip trailer); legacy 'g'/'r' tokens still decode. (2) Compact v2 payload (color table + char-per-peg) shrinks dense photo-import links: fully-filled 29x29 in 8 colors < 1200 chars vs multi-KB JSON, snowflake 365→282. Friendlier decode-error toast. 5 new tests (40 total): tail-safety sweep, legacy decode, v2 polar/dense round-trips, malformed rejection.

Import options panel with live re-run: colors slider (2-16), fit select, background-removal + drop-islands toggles, bead-count stats; Keep/Cancel semantics (Cancel deletes the created design; navigating away keeps last state). Fit modes in core samplePegs: contain (aspect-preserving letterbox, square default — fixes wide-image squash), cover (crop, polar default = historical behavior), stretch (legacy edge-to-edge). Out-of-image cell area counts as transparent so letterbox bands fall out of the alpha threshold naturally. CLI gains --fit; square CLI default changes stretch→contain (documented). 4 new fit tests (44 total); browser e2e verified: wide 2:1 image letterboxes in correct proportion with panel live.
Fix deployed (212e18e) and confirmed by Mike; 16px fields under pointer:coarse remove the iOS focus-zoom trigger
Deployed (eb7205d + c0672a7) and confirmed by Mike on-device, including the two-finger undo race
Deployed (4c6bff9) and confirmed by Mike on-device: pick, snap-back, and palette scroll all good
